statement.p: Is there a linear relationship in the table?

solution.p: The changes in x from one row to the next are +3\text{,}+2\text{,} and +8\text{.} Thats not a consistent pattern, but we need to consider rates of change between points. The rate of change between the first two points is \frac{-8-(-2)}{6-3}=-2\text{.} The rate of change between the next two points is \frac{-12-(-8)}{8-6}=-2\text{.} And the rate of change between the last two points is \frac{-20-(-12)}{12-8}=-2\text{.} So the rate of change, -2\text{,} is constant regardless of which pairs we choose. That means these pairs describe a linear relationship.
